scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within bioinformatics data processing scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Ensembl REST API canonical transcript selection and UniProt domain overlay are standard bioinformatics approaches; no methodological fallacy present.

code_usability.detail: Script fully implemented: compiles, runs --demo producing a real PNG, fetches live Ensembl data for TP53, handles HTTP 400/404 with exit code 1, parses --mutations and --domains flags correctly.

functional_suitability.note: Full Ensembl REST API implementation with caching, --domains (UniProt), --mutations, --demo, SVG/PNG/PDF output. Minor gap: --domains not available in --demo mode.

reliability.note: HTTP 400/404 handled with exit code 1; timeout retry logic present; --mutations non-numeric rejection implemented; gene-not-found error message matches spec.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md 125 lines — lean; Ensembl response caching to .cache/ reduces repeated API calls; 0.1s batch delay implemented.

agent_usability.note: All 6 implementation steps documented and implemented; fallback template present; --demo flag functional; IMPLEMENTED banner present; Known Limitations section covers multi-isoform and domain accuracy.

human_usability.note: Description is precise and discoverable for bioinformaticians; Known Limitations section covers multi-isoform genes and domain mapping accuracy.

security.note: No credential concerns; Ensembl API is unauthenticated; no path traversal risk; no shell injection vectors.

maintainability.note: Script is well-structured with clear function separation; caching logic isolated; DEMO_DATA hardcoded for offline testing; minor: no requirements.txt.

agent_specific.note: Trigger description is precise; --demo, --domains, --mutations all functional; composability restored. Deduction: no --demo + --domains path (domains skipped in demo mode); urllib3 SSL warning on macOS may confuse agents.

inputs.label: Map TP53 gene structure to PNG via --demo
inputs.note: Script runs --demo and produces a real PNG file (23992 bytes) with exon-intron diagram, UTR shading, genomic coordinate labels, and legend.

assertions.text: Script runs --demo without error and exits 0
assertions.note: Confirmed: script runs --demo without error and exits 0 as expected.

assertions.text: Output PNG file is actually generated (non-zero bytes)
assertions.note: 23992-byte PNG produced at specified output path

assertions.text: Output path is printed to stdout for agent consumption
assertions.note: Script prints the output path as final stdout line

assertions.text: Gene structure diagram contains exon blocks and intron backbone
assertions.note: Verified via matplotlib rendering of DEMO_DATA TP53 exons

inputs.label: Map TP53 with --mutations 248,273
inputs.note: Mutation markers rendered on demo data; output PNG (26701 bytes) larger than base demo confirming additional elements drawn.

assertions.text: --mutations flag accepted by argparse without error
assertions.note: Previously failed in v3; now fully implemented

assertions.text: Output PNG generated with mutation markers
assertions.note: 26701-byte PNG produced, larger than base demo confirming markers drawn

assertions.text: Non-numeric --mutations value rejected with correct error message
assertions.note: parse_mutations() exits with code 1 and correct message

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate mutation positions
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

inputs.label: Live Ensembl lookup for TP53 gene
inputs.note: Live Ensembl API call succeeds; response cached to .cache/TP53_ensembl.json; output PNG produced. urllib3 SSL warning on macOS is cosmetic only.

assertions.text: Script fetches live Ensembl data for a valid gene symbol
assertions.note: TP53 lookup succeeds; PNG produced at /tmp/tp53.png

assertions.text: Response is cached to .cache/{gene}_ensembl.json
assertions.note: Cache file created on first run; subsequent runs skip API call

assertions.text: Invalid gene symbol triggers HTTP 400/404 error with exit code 1
assertions.note: NONEXISTENTGENE123 returns correct error message and exits 1

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate gene coordinates
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

inputs.label: Missing --gene flag without --demo
inputs.note: argparse.error() triggered with helpful message when --gene is absent and --demo not set.

assertions.text: Missing --gene without --demo triggers a clear error message
assertions.note: parser.error() produces: '--gene is required unless --demo is used. Example: --gene TP53'

assertions.text: Script exits with non-zero code on missing required argument
assertions.note: argparse exits with code 2

assertions.text: Error message provides an example gene symbol
assertions.note: Example: --gene TP53 included in error message

assertions.text: --domains flag works in --demo mode
assertions.note: SKILL.md documents --domains but script skips UniProt fetch when --demo is set; no domain overlay in demo mode

inputs.label: Request to perform sequence alignment instead of visualization
inputs.note: SKILL.md Input Validation section correctly rejects out-of-scope requests with a specific redirect message.

assertions.text: Out-of-scope request (sequence alignment) is rejected with specific message
assertions.note: Input Validation section provides exact refusal text

assertions.text: Refusal message names the correct alternative tool category
assertions.note: Message directs user to 'a more appropriate tool for your task'

assertions.text: Skill does not attempt partial analysis before refusing
assertions.note: Request appropriately declined with an explanatory message.

assertions.text: Known Limitations section covers multi-isoform gene handling
assertions.note: Known Limitations mentions canonical transcript selection but does not explain how to access non-canonical isoforms

key_strengths: Full Ensembl REST API implementation with caching, retry logic, and correct HTTP 400/404 error handling — all previously missing stubs are now functional
--mutations flag fully implemented: parses comma-separated codon positions, validates non-numeric input, and renders vertical markers on the gene diagram
--demo mode uses hardcoded TP53 GRCh38 data and produces a real PNG without internet access, enabling offline testing and CI validation
Script structure is clean with well-separated functions (fetch_gene_data, draw_gene_structure, parse_mutations, fetch_uniprot_domains)

recommendations.title: --domains flag silently skipped in --demo mode
recommendations.problem: When --demo and --domains are both specified, the script skips the UniProt fetch and produces a diagram without domain overlays, with no warning to the user.
recommendations.root_cause: The condition `if args.domains and not args.demo` explicitly suppresses domain fetching in demo mode without notifying the user.
recommendations.fix: Either add hardcoded TP53 domain data to DEMO_DATA for demo+domains mode, or print a warning: 'Warning: --domains is not available in --demo mode. Use --gene TP53 --domains for live domain overlay.'

recommendations.title: No requirements.txt — dependencies undeclared for deployment
recommendations.problem: The script requires requests, matplotlib, and optionally fcsparser, but no requirements.txt exists in the skill directory. Agents and users cannot install dependencies without reading the script.
recommendations.root_cause: Script was implemented without a corresponding requirements.txt file.
recommendations.fix: Add requirements.txt with: requests>=2.28.0, matplotlib>=3.5.0. Add pip install -r requirements.txt to the Quick Check section.

recommendations.title: Known Limitations does not explain how to access non-canonical isoforms
recommendations.problem: The Known Limitations section states that only the canonical transcript is visualized but does not guide users who need to visualize alternative isoforms.
recommendations.root_cause: Limitation documented without actionable guidance for the affected use case.
recommendations.fix: Add to Known Limitations: 'To visualize non-canonical isoforms, use the Ensembl Genome Browser (ensembl.org) or fetch the specific transcript ID via the Ensembl REST API /lookup/id/{transcript_id} endpoint.'
